'''Fondorians''' were a [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] [[Near-Human]]s [[Species/Legends|species]] from [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]].

==Biology and appearance==
Fondorians were baseline [[Human/Legends|Humans]], though they were distinguished by their [[Color/Legends|purple]] [[Blood/Legends|blood]] and lack of body hair.

==Society and culture==
Fondorian society was stratified into [[Wikipedia:caste|castes]]. Low-caste Fondorians traditionally had only one name, their family name. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Zsinj]] inherited such a trait from his [[Zsinj's father|Fondorian father]], a [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]]. Social mobility was possible for low-caste Fondorians, however: before defecting to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], [[Hosh Hune]] received an apprenticeship in the [[Fondor Guild of Starshipwrights]] as a reward for his family's generations of service to the guild.

Upper-caste Fondorians were extremely intelligent and regal beings who believed themselves smarter than any other [[Species/Legends|species]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were very sharp thinkers and were able to manipulate business deals to suit them, and for this reason, Fondor was one of the wealthiest [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in the galaxy. One Fondorian was the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] defector, [[Oolth]]. Author [[Metrisse]] was Fondorian as well.
